div.head: The Ambassador in Italy (Long) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 42. I talked on the telephone from New York with the President just before I sailed and after the conference you, Morgenthau,Henry Morgenthau, Jr., Secretary of the Treasury. WallaceHenry A. Wallace, Secretary of Agriculture. and Oscar JohnsonApparently has reference to Oscar Johnston, manager of cotton pool for Agricultural Adjustment Administration. had with him on the subject of the possibilities of cotton trade with Italy. The President authorized me to pick up negotiations which had been informally broached to me immediately before my departure from Italy. I have taken the matter up with SuvichFulvio Suvich, Under Secretary of State in the Italian Ministry of Foreign Affairs. who has expressed a keen interest in this as well as in the forthcoming general negotiations and said that he would be prepared to begin definite conversations with me on the cotton trade within a few days. I have an appointment with Mussolini tomorrow and will pursue the subject.
